    4. Fuck "Followers"... Introducing "Audience Mindset"
</h2>

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><h2>If you're overwhelmed by social media you're doing it wrong.</h2><h2>People are convinced that things have to be hard. Don't accept that narrative. Social media is supposed to be fun! And when things are fun, they become easy.</h2><h2>Social media is like a musical instrument. You practice. You develop your style. And eventually you will be able to use the tools to play any tune you like. </h2><p><br></p><h1><strong>1. ATTENTION ADDICTION</strong></h1><p>First you should understand why people always want more followers.</p><p>They want attention.</p><p>They want validation.</p><p>And they see followers as a direct correlation to fulfilling that addiction.</p><p>Post some bullshit...</p><p>Then check your phone 140 times a day (That's an actual statistic. And it's sad.)</p><p>And that addiction leads to psychosis.</p><p>A warped view of reality.</p><p>You try to get followers on social media because it's easier than real life. It's the difference between NBA 2K20 and playing on a real court.</p><p>What would you have to do in the real world to get those same people to pay attention?</p><p>That would be a whole new ballgame, right?</p><p>That's why you're chicken shit of approaching strangers. The real stuff... (But we'll deal with that one soon, believe me...) </p><p>So instead, you try to attract strangers by being clever online. Where your ego is safer.</p><p>I know, I know... Some people have succeeded at making this online "game" a full time job. Especially when it was a new thing back in 2015.</p><p>We gave them a name.</p><p>Influencers. </p><p>But that's a lottery.</p><p>The Mystery School is not about creating influencers.</p><p>The Mystery School is about creating the people who influence the influencers. </p><p>Artists.</p><p>Before you try to "grow" on Instagram... Let's do some housecleaning. </p><p>From now on there will be less ego-stroking. And more "doing things with purpose". </p><p><br></p><h1><strong>2. AUDIENCE VS. FOLLOWERS</strong></h1><p>If you stopped posting today. Who are the people who would notice? Who are the people who would miss you? </p><p>The fans who like every post...</p><p>The ones leaving thoughtful comments...</p><p>The ones that, if you decide to sell something, they buy...</p><p>Who are the ones who would come to your "Instagram funeral"?</p><p>Those people are called your audience.</p><p>They are the only ones that matter.</p><p>And when you finally realize that... You've acquired the keys to your kingdom.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>3. NURTURE FIRST</strong></h1><p>Before you try to attract new people to your audience you need to nurture the ones you already have.</p><p>You do that by getting to know them.</p><p>If you make a new friend, or you start dating someone, the first thing you do is try to get to know their likes and dislikes etc... And you look for that common ground. Right?</p><p>The digital world is no different. </p><p>These are PEOPLE on the other end. Stop treating them like numbers.</p><p>Treat your Day 1s with care.</p><p>When you're too "big" to nurture your audience, you can hire fan clubs to do it for you... But for now... Do the "work".</p><p>Make it your duty to understand who your followers are. </p><h2>Like my friend Dana who stopped her IG growth on purpose. She put her IG profile on private so she could focus on her audience and service them correctly... and then went HAM in the DMs.</h2><p>That's the right energy.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>4. "REMOVE" followers</strong></h1><p>Instagram shows you the least engaged followers you have and lets you silently remove them. It also shows you your "Least Interacted With".</p><p>Use those tools.</p><p>Unfollow people that don't align with the new you.</p><p>Start fresh to make your IG more succinct and representative of who you are becoming.</p><p>Oliver (mystery student) went in...</p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/j8kEH1qTmO5ZbGmShjcc_oliveryes2.jpg" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/j8kEH1qTmO5ZbGmShjcc_oliveryes2.jpg" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"></p><h1><strong>5. DEFINE YOUR AUDIENCE</strong></h1><p>Your audience is not everybody. It isn't the masses.</p><p>It's a community that corals around you for a reason.</p><p>What is that reason?</p><p>Once you know this... You can proudly take the next step.</p><p>Oh, and it's such a powerful move:</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>6. PISS YOUR "FOLLOWERS" OFF... </strong></h1><p>Create a series of posts that are soooooo perfectly geared towards your core audience that everyone that doesn't fit in to your new IG's world view will quickly unfollow.</p><p>The double reaction you want is:</p><p>1. some people saying "fuck this. unfollow." and...</p><p>2. others will be like "Wow. I needed to see this today." </p><p>That's how you know that you're on the right track.</p><p>As mystery student @msthighlicious who does this on a regular basis would say... </p><p>"Good Riddance"</p><p>What if you concentrated all your energy into being so singular and hardcore about what you stand for that people were forced to either unfollow you or praise you for reading their mind?</p><p>Stop being afraid of pissing people off.</p><p>You're going to piss people off if you're doing anything decent. Accept this NOW.</p><p>It's ok to piss people off. It's good actually. It's engagement. Engagement that will lead to more people like the ones you helped already or that love your art... find you.</p><p>Haters are the perfect dog-whistle for attracting more of your true fans.</p><p>Piss your "followers" off... Gain an audience.</p><p>Remember the <a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/12084289" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Secondary Effects</a> module?</p><p>If you go-in 100% on your message, maybe some people will leave... But give it time... your fans will find you.</p><h2><em>Just like my friend Kate Noel who lost 5,000 followers after she started posting about her struggles with her weight. Before she did this house-cleaning, 80% of her followers were guys sending her dick pics. After house-cleaning, her audience was 60% women and a community of people interested in following her journey.</em></h2><h2><strong><em>Judge the power of your audience by how invested it is in your point of view. Not by how big it is.</em></strong></h2><p><br></p><h1><strong>7. PREPARE YOUR MIND FOR CONSISTENCY ("DROPPING BOMBS")</strong></h1><p>Now that you're in the right "audience" frame of mind... there's one last step.</p><p>Prepare your mind for the upcoming work.</p><p>If you can't be consistent, you're not ready to grow an audience.</p><p>We can't all be @playboicarti . </p><p>Only people that are consistent in the outside world don't need to be consistent on IG. But if IG is your main promotional hustle for getting your work known, then you'll need to start dropping bombs.</p><p>Dropping bombs doesn't mean only releasing controversial content.</p><p>It's just that what you post just needs to feel like "bombs" to your audience. It's whatever you've promised to deliver. It's the reason they came to you. It could be dope music. An insightful piece of writing. A resource that helps them.</p><p>But you need to give it to them on a schedule.</p><p>Don't try grow an audience that you can't service with that type of consistency.</p><p>That's why I'm mentioning it now. Get ready for it.</p><p>In 'The Book Of Influence' I'll be showing you how to make your online world a well-oiled machine. And then you can "scale".</p><p>But for now, prepare your mind for the relentless work of consistency.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>8. BETTER THAN HASHTAGS</strong></h1><p>Imagine you're a t-shirt company with 500 followers and an audience of 100 that buys every time you release something? Uh... You have a business!</p><p>An audience that cares about you and that shares what you with others will make you grow faster than any clever or thirsty hashtag could ever do.</p><p><em>“You don't need massive influencers to grow — you can truly do it if you have shareable content that people want to send to their friends.” Koreen (@werenotreallystrangers)</em></p><p><br></p><p></p>
</div>
